dashboard.algorithm_desc: Controls which Machine Learning model performs the RUL diagnosis
dashboard.avg_rul_tooltip: Remaining Useful Life: Average remaining cycles calculated by AI before requiring intervention.

engineDetail.confidence_tooltip: Probability certainty metric of the AI model regarding its RUL calculation.
engineDetail.temp_hpc_tooltip: Sensor 3 (T41): Total temperature at the high-pressure compressor outlet. A high value indicates loss of aerodynamic efficiency.
engineDetail.pressure_hpc_tooltip: Sensor 11: Static pressure at station 30. Tends to decay as the engine accumulates fatigue.
engineDetail.core_speed_tooltip: Sensor 14 (Nc): RPM of the main shaft. Alterations reflect excessive friction or seal failures.
engineDetail.temp_pressure_rpm_chart: HPC Temperature, HPC Pressure, Core RPM
engineDetail.xai_tooltip: SHAP values that mathematically explain how many cycles of life each individual sensor adds (green) or subtracts (red) to the estimated RUL.
engineDetail.no_shap_data: No XAI explainability data available for this prediction.

roi.title: ROI Analysis (Return on Investment)
roi.subtitle: Estimated financial impact of predictive maintenance
roi.note: Based on algorithmic performance (R²) and standard aviation industry operational costs.

glossary.title: Technical Documentation
glossary.subtitle: Knowledge base and definitions of system operating parameters.
glossary.rul_desc1: Remaining Useful Life (RUL) is the statistical projection of the number of operating cycles or the amount of time an equipment can continue to operate safely before the degradation of its components crosses a critical failure threshold.
glossary.rul_desc2: In this system, RUL is calculated using artificial intelligence models (Random Forest and Advanced Regression models) that analyze the history of over 20 thermodynamic sensors simultaneously, looking for fatigue patterns not detectable by the naked eye.
glossary.risk_healthy: Healthy (RUL > 50 cycles): Nominal degradation. Expected wear within design parameters.
glossary.risk_med: Medium Alert (RUL between 31 and 50 cycles): Minor thermal or mechanical deviations. Requires monitoring.
glossary.risk_high: High Alert (RUL between 16 and 30 cycles): Probabilistic failure imminent in the medium term. Schedule maintenance window.
glossary.risk_critical: Critical (RUL less than or equal to 15 cycles): Structural collapse or imminent performance failure. Requires emergency intervention.
glossary.mod2_desc: The most relevant sensors whose behavior directly influences the useful life model predictions are detailed below.
glossary.sensor3_desc: Measures the total temperature at the High-Pressure Compressor outlet (T41). A constant increase in this temperature indicates that the compressor is working harder to maintain the same pressure ratio, which is the primary indicator of aerodynamic efficiency loss or blade wear.
glossary.sensor11_desc: Measures static pressure at station 30 (Ps30). Its value tends to progressively decay as the engine accumulates thermal fatigue damage. A correlation of high temperature (Sensor 3) combined with low pressure (Sensor 11) immediately triggers an algorithmic reduction in the useful life calculation.
glossary.sensor14_desc: Represents the physical core shaft revolutions per minute (Nc). Abrupt deviations in vibrations or forced speed increases usually reflect excessive internal friction or main bearing seal failures.
glossary.sensor2_desc: Temperature at the Low-Pressure Compressor outlet (T24). It usually correlates with initial fatigue phenomena and obstructions in the air intakes ahead of the main combustion chamber.

settings.title: System Settings
settings.restart_warning: Interval changes require a backend server restart to take effect.
settings.system_info_desc: Versions and data of the production environment
settings.auto_desc: The system automatically selects the optimal model based on available history. Uses LSTM when >=30 cycles, Random Forest for the rest.
settings.rf_desc: Decision tree ensemble model. Fast, interpretable, and efficient. Works well with any amount of history cycles.
settings.lstm_desc: Long short-term memory recurrent neural network. Captures complex temporal degradation patterns, but requires at least 30 history cycles.

calendar.subtitle: Visualize scheduled maintenance and log new service windows for the engines.

reports.subtitle: Select an equipment to generate a detailed PDF report with its RUL history, alerts, and recent readings.
